title: macrosolen geminatus
intro: Macrosolen geminatus, also known as the Indian periwinkle vine or Manna vine, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and act as an antimicrobial agent. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating ulcers, and helping to manage diabetes. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to have a range of health benefits.
